title: BatchExecuted payload
description: Emitted once per work.batch call. Records the per-op rationales + outcome + the cascade event ids of any sub-ops that committed (cascade events have refs.caused_by_event_id pointing at this BatchExecuted's event_id). The envelope's entity carries the batch's own synthetic identity (kind='batch', slug='<span_id-prefix>'); per-op entities live in their own cascade events. continue_on_error mirrors the caller's flag — false (default) means abort-on-first-error with the whole outer tx rolled back; true means run every op regardless and report per-op status. rolled_back is true iff the outer tx rolled back (which only happens when continue_on_error=false AND failed>0). Per the T1 design audit (docs/WORK_BATCHING_AND_FORGE_TEMPLATES_PLAN.md §3.1), batch_rationale is recorded in addition to the envelope-level rationale so listeners can distinguish 'why this MCP call' from 'why these ops were grouped' when both are supplied.

op_count.description: Total ops the caller supplied. Always equals len(ops).

succeeded.description: Count of ops with ok=true. In default mode (continue_on_error=false), succeeded+failed equals the index of the failing op + 1, not op_count, because remaining ops never executed. In continue_on_error=true mode, succeeded+failed always equals op_count.

continue_on_error.description: Mirrors the caller-supplied flag (default false). false = abort on first error + outer-tx rollback. true = run every op regardless + each op commits independently in its own savepoint; failures land in ops[].error_* without rollback.

rolled_back.description: true iff the outer tx rolled back. Only possible when continue_on_error=false AND failed > 0. When true, all entries with ok=true in ops[] DID execute and emit cascade events, but those events did NOT commit — they are absent from the events table. When false, every ok=true entry's cascade event_id refers to a committed row.

batch_rationale.description: Optional 'why these ops were grouped' rationale, distinct from the envelope-level rationale ('why this MCP call'). Often duplicative; supplied when the caller wants to record both grains.

ops.description: Per-op outcome records, in caller-supplied order. ok=false entries carry error_kind + error_message; ok=true entries that committed carry event_id (the cascade event's id). When rolled_back=true, ok=true entries lack event_id because their cascade events were rolled back with the outer tx.

position.description: Zero-based index in the caller-supplied ops list.

ok.description: Whether the op's handler returned without error.

rationale.description: Verbatim per-op rationale from the caller. Required on every op per the dispatch-policy gate.

event_id.description: The cascade event's id. Present only when ok=true AND rolled_back=false (the cascade actually committed).

error_kind.description: Short error classifier (e.g. 'TaskNotFound', 'InvalidTransition', 'UnknownAction'). Present only when ok=false.

error_message.description: Full error message verbatim. Present only when ok=false.
